title: HTML Comments Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the syntax and rules for using comments in HTML.

questions.question: Which of these is the correct syntax for an HTML comment?
options: // this is a comment
/* this is a comment */
<!-- this is a comment -->
# this is a comment

questions.question: What happens when the browser encounters an HTML comment on a web page?
options: It displays the comment in a special font.
It executes any script inside the comment.
It completely ignores the content inside the comment.
It highlights the comment for search engine robots.

questions.question: Is it safe to store sensitive information (like passwords) in HTML comments since they aren't shown on the screen?
options: Yes, they are completely private.
No, anyone can read them by viewing the page source.
Only if you use a secure font for them.
Only for desktop users.

questions.question: What is 'commenting out' code useful for?
options: To make the code run much faster.
To temporarily hide a piece of HTML without deleting it for testing.
To add a specific design pattern from a library.
To automatically generate documentation.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a best practice for using comments in HTML?
options: Using them to mark the start and end of large sections.
Leaving private internal notes that could be seen by the public.
Keeping them concise and relevant.
Using them to explain non-obvious code decisions.
